title: Add scheduling fields to Job model
description: Add scheduledStart, scheduledEnd (nullable DateTime) and schedulingNote (nullable String) to the Job model. schedulingNote is required when both time fields are null — enforced by Zod on create and update. Display all three fields in the Dispatcher Jobs view and Driver My Jobs view. Run Prisma migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma schema adds scheduledStart DateTime?, scheduledEnd DateTime?, schedulingNote String? to Job model. Migration created and applied.

POST /api/jobs Zod schema: scheduledStart and scheduledEnd are optional ISO datetime strings. If both are omitted or null, schedulingNote is required (non-empty string). If either time is provided, schedulingNote is optional.
GET /api/jobs and GET /api/jobs/my responses include scheduledStart, scheduledEnd, schedulingNote on each job object.
JobsPage (dispatcher view) displays scheduled times formatted as Finnish datetime (e.g. 24.3.2026 14:00) or the schedulingNote text when times are absent.
MyJobsPage (driver view) displays the same scheduling info in each job row.
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors after implementation.
notes: DateTime fields must be stored as UTC. Frontend datetime-local inputs must call .toISOString() before sending. Finnish format helper: new Date(val).toLocaleString('fi-FI', { dateStyle: 'short', timeStyle: 'short' }). Do not add schedulingNote to the driver PATCH /api/jobs/:id/status endpoint — that is covered by a separate task.
